rules.name: Resources registered in <Refine>
rules.check: Every directory under src/resources/ must have a corresponding entry in App.tsx <Refine resources={[...]}>. Flag orphan resource directories.
rules.why: Resources outside the Refine list miss access control, breadcrumbs, i18n, and navigation integration.

rules.name: dataProvider is @refinedev/nestjs-query
rules.check: Flag App.tsx imports of dataProvider from anywhere other than @refinedev/nestjs-query (or a thin wrapper around it).
rules.why: The nestjs-query dataProvider knows how to translate Refine's filter/sort/paging shape to nestjs-query GraphQL operations.

rules.name: No direct fetch() for backend
rules.check: Flag fetch() / axios calls in src/ that target the backend API. Use Refine hooks (useList, useOne, useCreate, useCustom) or extend the dataProvider.
rules.why: Direct fetch bypasses auth token injection, cache invalidation, error normalization, and access control.

rules.name: authProvider wired to Firebase
rules.check: App.tsx authProvider must delegate login/logout/check to the project's Firebase service (src/lib/firebase.ts or src/providers/auth-provider.ts).
rules.why: Authoritative auth state must live in one place.

rules.name: accessControlProvider set
rules.check: App.tsx <Refine> must pass an accessControlProvider. Flag missing provider or provider that returns {can: true} unconditionally.
rules.why: Without a real provider, role-based UI gates silently render for all users.

rules.name: Inline gql tags (no .graphql files)
rules.check: Flag standalone .graphql / .gql files in src/. Operations must be inline gql`...` template tags in .ts/.tsx files.
rules.why: Project convention; codegen is configured to scan inline gql, not standalone files.

rules.name: TypedDocumentNode imported from codegen output
rules.check: Query hooks must consume typed operations generated in src/graphql/types.ts. Flag hooks passing raw gql strings without the generated TypedDocumentNode.
rules.why: Loses end-to-end type safety and silently accepts schema-breaking queries.

rules.name: Generated types never hand-edited
rules.check: Flag any git diff modifying src/graphql/types.ts or src/graphql/schema.types.ts without a corresponding code/schema change.
rules.why: Files are auto-generated; manual edits are overwritten on next codegen run.

rules.name: Codegen freshness before commit
rules.check: After any inline gql change in src/, codegen output must be regenerated and committed in the same PR. Flag PRs with gql changes but no src/graphql/*.ts diff.
rules.why: Stale generated types drift from operations.

rules.name: No anonymous operations
rules.check: Every gql tag must have a named operation (query Foo, mutation Bar). Flag gql`query { ... }` without a name.
rules.why: Named operations are required for codegen to emit typed hooks and for observability.

rules.name: Zod imported from 'zod/v4'
rules.check: Flag any import from 'zod' — all imports must be from 'zod/v4'.
rules.why: Project uses Zod v4 (separate import path). 'zod' resolves to v3 which breaks the custom error map.

rules.name: zodResolver wires react-hook-form
rules.check: Forms using react-hook-form must use useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) }) — flag forms without a resolver or with a custom resolver.
rules.why: Zod + resolver is the standard contract; custom resolvers bypass the form-validation error map.

rules.name: Custom form-validation error map used
rules.check: Forms must import and apply the error map from src/lib/form-validation.ts (typically via a top-level z.setErrorMap or schema .describe()). Flag schemas that silently fall back to Zod's default English errors.
rules.why: User-facing error messages need i18n keys, not Zod defaults.

rules.name: FormProvider wraps field components
rules.check: When custom field components use useFormContext(), the parent must wrap children in <FormProvider {...methods}>. Flag useFormContext calls with no FormProvider ancestor.
rules.why: Without FormProvider, useFormContext returns undefined and field registration silently fails.

rules.name: shadcn components from components/ui/
rules.check: Composite UI primitives (Button, Input, Dialog, etc.) must import from src/components/ui/. Flag direct Radix imports when a shadcn wrapper exists.
rules.why: shadcn wrappers apply project Tailwind classes and CSS variables; bypassing them breaks theme consistency.

rules.name: Tailwind 4 via @tailwindcss/vite
rules.check: vite.config.ts must import @tailwindcss/vite. Flag legacy postcss-based Tailwind config remnants.
rules.why: Tailwind 4 uses the Vite plugin; mixing with legacy PostCSS config produces broken builds.

rules.name: No hardcoded hex colors in components
rules.check: Flag className attributes containing literal hex like bg-[#xxxxxx], text-[#xxxxxx]; flag style props with hex color values.
rules.why: Brand tokens live in CSS variables (src/index.css). Hardcoded hex breaks theme and dark mode.
rules.fix: Use bg-primary, text-accent, or var(--primary) references.

rules.name: Emotion secondary to Tailwind+shadcn
rules.check: Flag widespread use of @emotion/styled as primary styling when shadcn/Tailwind equivalent exists. Allow Emotion only for legitimate cases (animations, complex keyframes) with a justifying comment.
rules.why: Two styling systems duplicate scopes and break theme-variable consumption.

rules.name: Icons from lucide-react
rules.check: Icons must come from lucide-react. Flag imports from @heroicons, react-icons, or inline SVG unless wrapped in a shadcn component.
rules.why: Single icon library keeps bundle size and visual style consistent.

rules.name: Firebase token injected in data-provider
rules.check: src/providers/data-provider.ts (or equivalent) must attach the Firebase ID token to outgoing requests via a fetch interceptor. Flag dataProvider setups missing token attachment.
rules.why: Backend requires Firebase ID token; missing it means 401 on every request.

rules.name: authProvider returns Firebase user
rules.check: authProvider.check / getIdentity must read from Firebase Auth state. Flag hardcoded user objects or localStorage-only auth state.
rules.why: Single source of truth for user identity.

rules.name: UserStateGuard wraps routed pages
rules.check: App.tsx must wrap protected routes with the project's user-state guard component (typically <UserStateGuard>). Flag routed pages outside the guard.
rules.why: Prevents flash-of-authenticated-content while Firebase rehydrates.

rules.name: No direct Firebase Auth calls in pages
rules.check: Flag signInWithEmailAndPassword / signOut / onAuthStateChanged calls inside src/resources/ or src/pages/. These belong in src/lib/firebase.ts and the authProvider.
rules.why: Centralized auth lifecycle prevents race conditions between pages.

rules.name: useTranslation from @refinedev/core
rules.check: Flag any import of useTranslation from 'react-i18next' — must come from '@refinedev/core'.
rules.why: Refine proxies useTranslation through its i18nProvider to translate resource names, actions, and notifications consistently.

rules.name: Key prefix convention
rules.check: Translation keys used in resource pages must follow pages.<resource>.<section>.<key>. Flag keys that don't match this shape in src/resources/.
rules.why: Predictable key layout makes translations searchable and prevents collisions.

rules.name: No inline English strings
rules.check: User-visible strings must be wrapped in t(...). Flag JSX text nodes longer than 2 words that aren't inside a t() call.
rules.why: Hardcoded strings block translations.

rules.name: No missing translation keys
rules.check: Every t('...') key used in code must exist in the i18n source files. Flag unresolved keys that would render as the key string itself.
rules.why: Missing keys render raw in the UI.

rules.name: import.meta.env.VITE_* only
rules.check: Flag any process.env.* reference in src/. Use import.meta.env.VITE_* with types in src/vite-env.d.ts.
rules.why: process.env doesn't exist in the Vite browser bundle; use fails at runtime.

rules.name: Env types declared in vite-env.d.ts
rules.check: src/vite-env.d.ts must declare interface ImportMetaEnv with every VITE_* var used in the codebase. Flag untyped env access.
rules.why: Typed env catches typos at compile time.

rules.name: No committed .env files
rules.check: Flag .env, .env.production, .env.local, etc. committed to git. Only .env.example (with empty values) may be committed.
rules.why: Env files often contain secrets.

rules.name: src/graphql/types.ts never hand-edited
rules.check: Flag any modification to src/graphql/types.ts not accompanied by gql operation or schema changes + codegen run in the same commit.
rules.why: Auto-generated file; hand edits are overwritten on next codegen.

rules.name: src/graphql/schema.types.ts never hand-edited
rules.check: Same as FE-PROT-001 but for schema.types.ts.
rules.why: Auto-generated file.

rules.name: Generated files have codegen header
rules.check: src/graphql/*.ts must retain the generator header comment. Flag files where the header has been removed.
rules.why: Header signals 'do not edit'; removing it invites edits.

rules.name: patches/ tied to package.json
rules.check: Any change under patches/ must be accompanied by a matching package.json patchedDependencies entry (or bun patch workflow). Flag orphan patch files or unused entries.
rules.why: Unlinked patches don't apply on fresh install.

rules.name: Only bun.lock committed
rules.check: Flag presence of package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml. Only bun.lock (or bun.lockb) allowed.
rules.why: Mixed lockfiles cause install drift.

rules.name: CI uses bun commands
rules.check: Flag npm install / yarn / pnpm install in .github/workflows/*.yml. Use bun install and bun run <script>.
rules.why: CI must match dev environment runtime.

rules.name: Scripts run via bun run
rules.check: Flag README / CLAUDE.md instructions referencing npm/yarn/pnpm commands for this project. Should read 'bun run ...'.
rules.why: Consistent docs prevent developer confusion.

rules.name: .test.ts/.test.tsx naming
rules.check: Frontend tests must use Vitest's .test.ts / .test.tsx naming. Flag .spec.ts files in src/ (that's Jest/backend convention).
rules.why: Vitest config discovers .test.* by default; .spec.* may be skipped.

rules.name: React Testing Library patterns
rules.check: Component tests must query via screen.getByRole / getByLabelText / getByText. Flag tests using .querySelector, container queries, or CSS-class selectors.
rules.why: Accessibility-first queries ensure tests verify user-visible behavior.

rules.name: MSW for GraphQL mocks
rules.check: GraphQL in tests must be mocked via msw/node handlers. Flag tests that mock the GraphQL client directly (e.g., jest.mock('@refinedev/nestjs-query')).
rules.why: MSW mocks at the network layer; client mocks miss retry/cache behavior.

rules.name: Coverage floor on changed lines
rules.check: Changed lines should have >= 60% test coverage. Severity info until Vitest harness is installed project-wide.
rules.why: Coverage trend protects against untested regressions.
